title: betula × minor
intro: Betula × minor, also known as the hybrid birch, is a cross between the paper birch (Betula papyrifera) and the river birch (Betula nana), resulting in a unique and versatile plant species. The medicinal properties of Betula × minor lie in its bark, which contains compounds like betulin and betulinic acid that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of using Betula × minor include reducing fever, relieving skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and supporting the immune system due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Traditionally, the bark of Betula × minor has been used in herbal preparations like teas, tinctures, and salves to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
